name: Josh Jacobs
headline: Elite goal-line volume props up a fading efficiency profile
body:
Jacobs remains a touchdown machine even as the underlying numbers slip. He punched in 13 rushing scores across 15 games in 2025 on the back of 58 red-zone opportunities and 20 goal-line carries, but the efficiency cratered: 3.97 yards per carry, a -0.011 EPA per play, and just 1.82 yards after contact per attempt, all sharp declines from his 4.42-clip, 1,329-yard 2024. His broken-tackle rate collapsed too, from one every 9.1 carries to one every 19.5.

The fantasy case is volume, not explosiveness. He still owns Green Bay's backfield and red-zone work, and 36 catches give a modest PPR floor, but only 4.27% of his runs went for breakaways. He turns 28 with a knee and calf history, and off-field uncertainty clouds his availability. Bank on the touchdowns and treat him as a high-floor RB2 rather than the RB5 he was two years ago.
